title: Things you thought the manga did better
display_title: Things you thought the manga did better
wikitext:
[]: Things you thought the manga did better?             Quote Post by Dataprime » Thu Feb 12, 2015 4:35 pm I wanted to make this topic for a long while and I figured now is the best time to do it! So in honor of the final volume reaching the Americas I ask you this: Do you think any particular scene(s) were handled better in the manga than in the anime? If so, what parts?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Thu Feb 12, 2015 4:54 pm It didn't drop the ball on Shinji/Rei until about the halfway point (as opposed to 1/4 the way in like the anime).

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Thu Feb 12, 2015 5:21 pm I really like the idea of depicting snow in Eva-Verse.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Thu Feb 12, 2015 6:44 pm When it tried to be funny, it actually succeeded.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Thu Feb 12, 2015 7:05 pm - Kaji's past and motivations for finding the truth, since it actually gave one to him unlike in the TV series. - Toji's death, made worse by the fact that Shinji knew that he was the pilot of EVA-03 and even told him that the cockpit of an Eva is the safest place in the world to reassure him, that was a truly efficient gut punch. - the MP-Evas, instead of attacking one by one and staying passive most of the time like in EoE, in the manga they attacked relentlessly and at once, really behaving like a pack of wild animals. - Ritsuko managing to shoot Gendo on the throat as a last act of spite, fucking glorious. - Yui's motivations better explained than in the anime.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Thu Feb 12, 2015 7:15 pm - Shinji being more angry and throwing a punch at Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Feb 12, 2015 7:29 pm Everything looks better and is better drawn than in the anime, whose quality varied with certain episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Ray » Thu Feb 12, 2015 7:45 pm Stillborn wrote: - Shinji . . . throwing a punch at Gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by outsane_geek » Thu Feb 12, 2015 7:57 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Everything looks better and is better drawn than in the anime, whose quality varied with certain episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Thu Feb 12, 2015 9:32 pm Ray wrote: -Sadamoto actually killed off Touji. Which really makes Shinji's descent into madness all the more believable.

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionGodMode » Fri Feb 13, 2015 12:45 am Kaworu was introduced earlier which would of been more a surprise to find out he was an Angel

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Feb 13, 2015 6:59 am Most of what's in this thread is great, but Ray wrote: An ending that while flawed, isn't a complete grimdark despair fest like EOE.

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Fri Feb 13, 2015 7:07 am Ray wrote: -Sadamoto actually killed off Touji. Which really makes Shinji's descent into madness all the more believable.

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Fri Feb 13, 2015 8:07 am Tumbling Down wrote: I strongly disagree. Shinji's descent into complete despair doesn't make sense without the comasturbation. That's supposed to be the final nail in his (mental) coffin. Without it, Shinji's desire to become nothing is sudden and forced.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Fri Feb 13, 2015 8:52 am Tumbling Down wrote: When it tried to be funny, it actually succeeded.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Fri Feb 13, 2015 1:46 pm Guy Nacks wrote: Everything looks better and is better drawn than in the anime, whose quality varied with certain episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Fri Feb 13, 2015 2:00 pm The reason that it's better drawn doesn't negate the fact that it is.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Feb 13, 2015 2:27 pm Na7e wrote: That's a little unfair considering it took Sadamoto twenty years to finish his adaptation, I would be shocked if it came out looking like shit.

[]:             Quote Post by Tumbling Down » Fri Feb 13, 2015 4:14 pm UrsusArctos wrote: C'mon, don't tell me you didn't see the blatant reactions of shock in Episode 08 when Kaji asked the same question! (And Shinji was oblivious to both the salacious question and to the horrified reactions around him)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Feb 13, 2015 5:47 pm Mr. Tines wrote: It didn't drop the ball on Shinji/Rei until about the halfway point (as opposed to 1/4 the way in like the anime).

So in honor of the final volume reaching the Americas I ask you this: Do you think any particular scene(s) were handled better in the manga than in the anime? If so, what parts?
